# Feature: Deliver AI-generated files back to the originating chat

## Problem

When a user asks Claude or Codex to generate an image, produce a PDF, write a document, or output any file, the LLM saves it to disk but the bridge only sends back the text response. The file is never delivered to the WhatsApp or Signal chat that requested it.

## Solution overview

Detect files created during an LLM turn and send them as media attachments immediately after the text reply. The detection mechanism must:
- Work for both Claude and Codex (Codex has no hook system)
- Require no prompt engineering or LLM cooperation
- Add no background processes or persistent state
- Be abstracted behind an interface so the implementation can be swapped

## Detection design: before/after snapshot

Before the LLM runs, take a snapshot of the chat dir (`store/chats/<chatJID>/`). After the LLM finishes, take another snapshot and diff the two. Files that appear in the second snapshot but not the first are new output files. Send each one as a media attachment.

This is **not** continuous scanning. It is two `os.ReadDir()` calls — one before, one after — per message processed. Cost is microseconds. No background goroutine, no timer, no watcher process, no persistent "already-sent" list.

The per-chat dir already isolates each chat's working space, so there is no cross-chat interference.

## Go interface

```go
// OutputTracker detects files created by an LLM during a single turn.
// Call Before() immediately before invoking the LLM.
// Call NewFiles() immediately after; it returns absolute paths of new output files.
type OutputTracker interface {
    Before() error
    NewFiles() ([]string, error)
}
```

## SnapshotTracker implementation

```go
// SnapshotTracker implements OutputTracker via two os.ReadDir calls.
type SnapshotTracker struct {
    dir    string
    before map[string]struct{} // set of filenames present before the run
}

func NewSnapshotTracker(dir string) *SnapshotTracker

func (t *SnapshotTracker) Before() error        // reads dir, stores filename set
func (t *SnapshotTracker) NewFiles() ([]string, error) // diffs, filters by extension, returns abs paths
```

Output file extension whitelist (files outside this list are ignored):
`.png .jpg .jpeg .webp .gif .mp4 .pdf .docx .xlsx .pptx .zip .html .svg .csv`

Internal files to always exclude regardless of extension:
`CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `whisper_prompt.json`, anything under `.claude/`

## OS-specific subclasses (future)

The `OutputTracker` interface is intentionally designed for future OS-native implementations:

| Implementation | Mechanism | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `SnapshotTracker` | `os.ReadDir` before/after diff | ✅ MVP |
| `InotifyTracker` (Linux) | inotify kernel events | Future |
| `FSWatcherTracker` (Windows) | `ReadDirectoryChangesW` | Future |
| `KqueueTracker` (macOS) | kqueue/FSEvents | Future |

The native watchers would be more efficient for very high-frequency chats, but the snapshot approach is sufficient for WhatsApp/Signal message volumes and adds no dependencies. The interface guarantees any implementation is a drop-in replacement.

## Integration in shared.go

```go
// handleWithClaude / handleWithCodex — pseudocode
tracker := NewSnapshotTracker(chatDirPath)
tracker.Before()

// ... run Claude or Codex CLI as today ...

sendFn(resp.Result) // send text reply as today

// send any generated files
if files, err := tracker.NewFiles(); err == nil {
    for _, path := range files {
        sendMediaFn(path)
    }
}
```

`sendMediaFn func(path string)` is a new parameter passed in by the caller (whatsapp.go / signal.go), alongside the existing `sendFn func(text string)`.

In `whatsapp.go`:
```go
sendMediaFn := func(path string) {
    sendWhatsAppMessage(client, chatJID, "", path)
}
```

In `signal.go`:
```go
sendMediaFn := func(path string) {
    sendSignalMessage(chatID, "", path)
}
```

## Files to create / modify

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `output_tracker.go` | New file: `OutputTracker` interface + `SnapshotTracker` implementation |
| `shared.go` | Add `sendMediaFn` parameter to `handleWithClaude` and `handleWithCodex`; add tracker calls |
| `whatsapp.go` | Pass `sendMediaFn` closure when calling `handleWithClaude` / `handleWithCodex` |
| `signal.go` | Same as whatsapp.go |

## Notes

- The system prompt in CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md should advise the LLM to save output files to the current working directory (relative paths). This covers the edge case of a script that might otherwise write to an absolute path outside the chat dir.
- Codex uses `-s workspace-write` with `cmd.Dir = chatDirPath`, so relative-path writes land in chatDir automatically.
- Both LLMs already use the same chatDirPath, so `SnapshotTracker` logic is identical for both.
